project: Amigos do Trabalho - Desktop Virtual Pet
description: Menu & HUD Overhaul, Shop & Inventory System, Settings, and Pet Enhancements - Redesigned coin HUD, bulk purchases, inventory, furniture removal, settings, pet selection, mood speech bubbles, and held animation.

userStories.title: Coin HUD redesign - styled panel with icon and animated counter
userStories.description: As a player, I want a game-style coin display (icon + animated counter, no label) positioned next to the menu toggle button so the HUD feels polished and integrated.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace CoinLabel with a PanelContainer containing an HBoxContainer with a coin icon (TextureRect with placeholder yellow circle) and a numeric Label
No 'Coins:' text label — only the icon and the number
White/light background with rounded corners (StyleBoxFlat)
Positioned directly to the left of the menu toggle button at the bottom-right
Counter animates on value change using a Tween that counts up/down over ~0.3s
Coin HUD moves upward smoothly when the slide menu opens (same tween timing as menu panel)
Returns to original position when menu closes
Included in the mouse passthrough polygon via a get_rect() helper method
CoinLabel node and all old coin label positioning code removed from main.gd and main.tscn
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is a UI-only change. The CoinSystem node and signals remain unchanged — only the display changes. The coin HUD should be a sibling of SlideMenu or a child of a shared bottom-right anchor. Use slide_menu.gd's get_toggle_rect() to position relative to the toggle button.

userStories.title: Shop window positioning adjacent to menu with chained animation
userStories.description: As a player, I want the shop window to appear next to the menu in a smooth visual sequence so the UI feels cohesive.
acceptanceCriteria: Shop panel appears adjacent to (left of) the slide menu panel, not overlapping it
Shop opens with a tween animation that starts after a slight delay (~0.1s) following the menu open
Shop closes before or simultaneously with the menu when the menu is closed
If the menu is closed while the shop is open, the shop closes first then the menu slides closed
Shop panel is included in the mouse passthrough polygon when visible
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This may require refactoring shop.gd's show/hide logic to use tweened positioning instead of simple visibility toggling. The shop panel's X position should be calculated relative to the menu panel's left edge.

userStories.title: Add discard_refund_ratio to FurnitureData and inventory data structure
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the data model foundations for inventory and discard before building the UI.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `discard_refund_ratio: float` export var to FurnitureData (default 0.5)
Update all existing .tres files in furniture/data/ with discard_refund_ratio values (table: 0.5, sofa: 0.5, food_bowl: 0.4, toy: 0.4, bookshelf: 0.6)

Add inventory management functions to main.gd or a new inventory_system.gd: add_to_inventory(furniture_id, quantity), remove_from_inventory(furniture_id, quantity), get_inventory_count(furniture_id), get_all_inventory()
Inventory stored as Dictionary[String, int] mapping furniture_id to quantity
Inventory persisted in save_data.json under 'inventory' key
Inventory loads on startup and saves on changes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the data layer only — no UI yet. The inventory system should be a separate node/script (inventory_system.gd) similar to coin_system.gd, emitting signals like inventory_changed(furniture_id, new_count) for UI binding.

userStories.title: Bulk purchase with quantity selector in shop
userStories.description: As a player, I want to buy multiple units of the same furniture item at once so I can furnish my space efficiently.
acceptanceCriteria: Each shop item row includes a quantity selector: minus button, quantity label (default 1), plus button
Minimum quantity is 1, no maximum
Total cost displayed dynamically next to the Buy button (unit price × quantity)
Buy button purchases the selected quantity if player has enough coins
Purchased items go to inventory via inventory_system (not immediate placement)
Remove the 'Owned' state — items can always be purchased again (no single-purchase limit)
Coins deducted for full quantity on purchase
The shop emits a signal or calls inventory_system to add items
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-022 for inventory_system. Remove the _owned array and related logic from shop.gd. The shop no longer tracks ownership — it just sells items that go into inventory.

userStories.title: Inventory panel UI with place and discard actions
userStories.description: As a player, I want an inventory panel to view stored furniture, place items, and discard unwanted ones for a partial refund.
acceptanceCriteria: New 'Inventory' button added to the slide menu (between Shop and Edit Layout)
Inventory panel opens adjacent to menu (same positioning pattern as shop panel)
Panel displays all inventory items with: icon, name, quantity, Place button, Discard button
Items with 0 quantity are hidden from the list
Clicking 'Place' triggers placement mode for that furniture item (decrements inventory count by 1 on successful placement)
Clicking 'Discard' shows a confirmation with the refund amount (coin_cost × discard_refund_ratio)
Confirming discard: credits coins, decrements inventory count by 1
Inventory panel included in mouse passthrough polygon when visible
Inventory and Shop cannot be open simultaneously — opening one closes the other
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-022 (inventory system) and US-023 (shop sends to inventory). Place action reuses existing placement mode in main.gd but sourced from inventory instead of shop. Discard needs a simple confirmation popup (ConfirmationDialog or custom panel).

userStories.title: Furniture removal in edit mode with Save Edit button
userStories.description: As a player, I want to remove placed furniture during edit mode and send it to inventory, with a clear Save Edit button.
acceptanceCriteria: Each furniture piece shows a remove button (X icon) above or beside it when edit mode is active
Clicking the remove button removes the furniture from the scene and adds it to inventory (increments count by 1)
If the pet was standing on the removed furniture, pet enters FALLING state
The 'Edit Layout' button changes label to 'Save Edit' during edit mode (or a new prominent button appears)
Clicking 'Save Edit' exits edit mode and persists all changes (positions + removals)
Pressing Escape also exits edit mode and persists changes (existing behavior preserved)
Remove buttons are included in mouse passthrough polygon during edit mode
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-022 (inventory system). The remove button can be a small Button node added as a child of the furniture scene dynamically when edit mode starts, removed when edit mode ends. Pet falling: check if pet's current surface furniture was removed and force FALLING state.

userStories.title: Store-to-inventory chest icon during placement mode
userStories.description: As a player, I want to store a furniture item directly into inventory during placement mode instead of placing it.
acceptanceCriteria: A chest/inventory icon button is visible in a fixed UI position during placement mode
Clicking the chest icon cancels placement and sends the item to inventory (no coin refund — item is kept)
Right-click during placement also sends the item to inventory instead of refunding coins
Escape during placement also sends the item to inventory instead of refunding coins
The old refund-on-cancel behavior is fully removed
Chest icon button included in passthrough polygon during placement mode
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-022 (inventory system). Modifies existing placement mode logic in main.gd. The key change: cancel no longer refunds coins, it stores to inventory. The chest button is an additional explicit way to do the same thing.

userStories.title: Settings panel with language selection
userStories.description: As a player, I want to select my preferred language from a settings menu so the game displays text in my language.
acceptanceCriteria: 'Settings' button in slide menu replaces the 'Settings (coming soon)' placeholder label
Settings panel opens adjacent to menu (same positioning pattern as shop/inventory)
Language section with a dropdown (OptionButton) listing available languages: English, Português
Create translation CSV file at res://translations/ with keys for all UI strings (menu buttons, shop labels, etc.)
Changing language calls TranslationServer.set_locale() and updates all UI text immediately
Selected language persisted in save_data.json under 'language' key
Language loaded and applied on startup
Settings panel included in mouse passthrough polygon when visible
Opening Settings closes Shop and Inventory panels
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use Godot's built-in TranslationServer with CSV translation files. All hardcoded UI strings need to be replaced with tr() calls. The settings panel script should be similar in structure to shop.gd.

userStories.title: Settings - monitor selection
userStories.description: As a player, I want to choose which monitor the game renders on so I can position my desktop pet on my preferred screen.
acceptanceCriteria: Monitor selection section added to the existing settings panel (below language)
Dropdown (OptionButton) lists all connected displays using DisplayServer.screen_get_count()
Each entry shows: 'Monitor N — WxH' (e.g., 'Monitor 1 — 1920×1080')
Selecting a monitor calls DisplayServer.window_set_current_screen(index) to move the window
Floor Y is recalculated via _compute_floor_y() after monitor change
All placed furniture repositioned: clamped to new screen bounds, Y set to new floor_y
Pet position clamped to new screen bounds
Selected monitor index persisted in save_data.json under 'monitor' key
Falls back to primary monitor (index 0) if saved monitor index exceeds screen_get_count()
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-027 (settings panel exists). DisplayServer.window_set_current_screen() moves the window. After moving, must recompute floor_y and reposition everything. Consider that screen sizes differ between monitors.

userStories.title: Pet selection UI driven by SpriteFrames resources
userStories.description: As a player, I want to select my pet from a menu so I can choose my companion.
acceptanceCriteria: 'Select Pet' button added to the slide menu
Pet selection panel opens with a scrollable grid layout (GridContainer inside ScrollContainer)
Pets discovered dynamically by scanning res://pet/ directory for .tres SpriteFrames files using DirAccess
Each pet entry shows: preview sprite (first frame of 'idle' animation from the SpriteFrames) and pet name (derived from filename without extension, capitalized)
UI scales to any number of pets — scrollable grid with consistent thumbnail sizes
Selecting a pet swaps pet_sprite.sprite_frames on the AnimatedSprite2D node
Currently selected pet has a visible highlight/border
Selected pet persisted in save_data.json under 'selected_pet' key (stores filename without extension)
Ozzy is the default if no selection is saved or saved pet file is missing
Pet selection panel included in mouse passthrough polygon when visible
Opening pet selection closes Shop, Inventory, and Settings panels
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The pet.gd currently builds SpriteFrames from a sprite_sheet texture via _setup_animations(). When swapping pets, load the .tres SpriteFrames directly and assign to sprite_frames property, bypassing _setup_animations(). May need to refactor pet.gd to support both modes (sprite sheet setup vs pre-built SpriteFrames).

userStories.title: Pet mood speech bubble with context-aware frequency
userStories.description: As a player, I want my pet to show mood via image-based speech bubbles so I can understand how it feels without color tinting.
acceptanceCriteria: Speech bubble node added as a child of PetSprite: a small Panel with a TextureRect for the mood image
Bubble positioned above the pet's head, follows pet movement
Mood images loaded from assets directory (placeholder icons: happy face, neutral face, sad/hungry face — can be simple colored circles initially)
Bubble appears on mood change events (coin threshold crossings between SAD/NEUTRAL/HAPPY)
Bubble appears after interactions (feed, play, sleep) showing the resulting mood
For SAD/hungry mood: bubble reappears at random intervals between 15-45 seconds as a reminder
For other moods: bubble only appears on change or interaction, not recurring
Bubble auto-hides after 3-5 seconds with a fade-out tween (modulate alpha 1.0 → 0.0)
Remove ALL existing modulate color changes from pet mood system (no more blue tint for SAD, green for HAPPY)
Pet sprite modulate stays at Color(1,1,1,1) at all times regardless of mood
Bubble included in mouse passthrough polygon when visible
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The current mood system in pet.gd applies modulate tints — these must be completely removed. The speech bubble replaces all visual mood feedback. Use a Timer node for the random SAD reminder interval. The bubble should use Tween for fade animations.

userStories.title: Pet dragged animation - exclusive animation for being held
userStories.description: As a player, I want my pet to play a unique 'dragged' animation when I'm holding/dragging it so the interaction feels alive.
acceptanceCriteria: When pet enters DRAGGED state, play the 'dragged' animation (already named 'dragged' in current code)
Create a distinct 'dragged' animation in the SpriteFrames that is visually different from idle (e.g., different row or programmatic variant)
'dragged' animation loops while the pet is held
On release (DRAGGED → FALLING), reverts to 'fall' animation
For Ozzy's sprite sheet: define 'dragged' animation using a dedicated row (row index configurable via export var held_row/dragged_row)
The _setup_animations() method builds the 'dragged' animation from its own sprite sheet row
If the dragged row is not available or has no frames, falls back to 'idle'
Works with any pet SpriteFrames — animation name convention is 'dragged'
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The animation is named 'dragged' (not 'held'). Currently dragged reuses idle frames — this story makes it a distinct animation from its own sprite sheet row. Update _setup_animations() to build 'dragged' from a configurable dragged_row export var. The _change_state() DRAGGED case already plays 'dragged', so the main work is making the animation visually distinct.
